articles.title: Can Bubbly Water Help You Lose Weight?
articles.summary: Sparkling water is a fizzy drink that some people think might help with weight loss. This article says it could make your body use sugar from food a little faster, which might help you feel less hungry. But the effect is very small. Scientists found that drinking bubbly water alone is not enough to help you lose a lot of weight. The author explains that to stay healthy and manage weight, you need to eat good foods and exercise regularly. Also, drinking too much sparkling water might upset your stomach. So, while it can be a fun drink, it's not a magic solution for losing weight.

articles.title: Scientists Find a Natural Hormone That Helps Mice Lose Weight
articles.summary: Scientists have found a natural hormone called FGF21 that can help mice lose weight by working in the brain. This hormone sends signals to a part of the brain called the hindbrain, which helps control how the body uses energy and how hungry the mice feel. It is similar to some weight loss medicines that people use. The researchers were surprised because they thought the hormone would work in a different brain area. By understanding how FGF21 works, scientists hope to create new treatments for obesity and liver diseases in the future. This discovery could lead to better ways to help people stay healthy without bad side effects.

articles.title: A European Rover's Journey to Mars
articles.summary: A European rover named Rosalind Franklin is finally getting a ride to Mars after many delays and changes. It was first planned to launch in 2009 with Russia's help, but that didn't happen. Then, NASA agreed to help, but they backed out in 2012 because of money problems. Europe then worked with Russia again, but when Russia invaded Ukraine in 2022, Europe stopped the partnership. Now, NASA is stepping in to launch the rover on a SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket, maybe in 2028. The rover will look for signs of life on Mars by studying the soil. This story shows how space missions can face big challenges but keep going with help from different countries.

articles.title: Astronauts Become Best Friends on Space Trip
articles.summary: Four astronauts went on a special space mission called Artemis II. They flew far away from Earth, around the Moon, and came back as best friends. The astronauts are Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen. They said their trip made people around the world feel happy and united. They wanted to show that going to the Moon is possible and can bring everyone together. The astronauts thanked everyone who helped build their spaceship. They shared stories about laughing, crying, and feeling connected. This mission reminded them that being human means caring for each other, no matter where we live.

articles.title: OpenAI Makes a Smart Computer for Biology
articles.summary: OpenAI has created a new computer program called GPT-Rosalind that helps scientists with biology. It is named after Rosalind Franklin, a famous scientist. This program is special because it is trained to understand biology tasks, like how genes work or how proteins are made. It can read lots of scientific papers and data to help researchers find important information faster. For example, if a scientist is studying a gene in the brain, GPT-Rosalind can explain brain science terms. OpenAI made it to be careful and not always agree with people, so it might say if an idea is not good. Right now, only some people in the United States can use it because OpenAI wants to make sure it is safe. Other companies have similar programs, but this one is just for biology.

articles.title: Scientists Help Mice with Sick Livers by Removing Zombie Cells
articles.summary: Scientists at UCLA found special cells called zombie cells that build up in the liver and cause damage. These cells are like broken cars that block traffic and make tissues sick. In mice with fatty liver disease from eating unhealthy food, removing these zombie cells made the liver healthier and smaller. The scientists used a drug to target only the zombie cells, and the mice got better even without changing their diet. This research shows that cleaning out these bad cells can help reverse liver problems, which might help people with similar diseases in the future.
